page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton4 processor family utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 design running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 256 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is supported by a 15 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights exceptional memory subsystem performance, with memory bandwidth for large block read and write operations ranking in the top tier. Passmark memory and database operations scores are also in the top tier, reflecting high efficiency for data-intensive workloads. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by top-tier Geekbench multi-core results and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in specific workloads, such as large-model LLM inference prompt processing and small-model text generation, which fall into the lower performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gd.8xlarge offers a highly dense resource profile by combining high-capacity memory with high-speed local NVMe storage on an energy-efficient ARM architecture. This configuration reduces the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput tasks. The server is highly suited for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads, including large-scale databases, Redis caching layers, data compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256 GB of system memory and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its memory bandwidth and database operations in the top tier, while multi-core processing remains highly competitive. However, it shows lower efficiency in large-scale LLM inference tasks. Qualitatively, the server provides high resource density by pairing substantial memory capacity with fast local storage on an ARM architecture. This profile makes the r8gd.8xlarge highly suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server with 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance combining dedicated Graviton cores with high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive and database workloads.

Equipped with 256 GB of system memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
